q: The intensity level of the threshold of hearing is _______ dB.
The frequency of an ultrasound is higher than _______ Hz.
The sound produced by the dog whistle is _______.
Another name for the quality of a sound is the _______.
The SI unit of Young's modulus is _______.
Echolocation is the special ability to emit sounds and interpret the _______.
The Doppler ultrasound is also known as _______.
In a liquid medium, the speed of sound is _______ proportional to the square-root of the bulk modulus of the medium.
The pitch of the note is determined by its _______.
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the _______ reaching an eardrum measured in W m^{-2}.
The frequency of infrasound is lower than _______ Hz.
The intensity of a wave is _______ proportional to the square of the amplitude of the wave.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit of audible frequency is _______ Hz.
The Doppler effect is used in some radars, to measure the _______ of the detected objects.
The minimum intensity of sound that can be heard by human is _______ W m^{-2}.
In a gas medium, the speed of sound is _______ proportional to the pressure in the gas.
The loudness of the note depends upon the _______ of the wave.
For every 10 dB added to the sound level, the loudness of the sound is increased by _______ times.
Radar is a form of _______ that uses electromagnetic waves.
For every 20 dB added to the sound level, the loudness of the sound is increased by _______ times.
The walls are covered up with _______ to reduce the effect of echo.
Young's modulus and bulk modulus are _______ properties of the medium.
The SI unit of bulk modulus is _______.
Sound propagates through _______ media such as air or water.
The _______ of the medium determines the rate at which sound is attenuated.
If the amplitude is increased 2 times, the loudness increases _______ times.
In a solid medium, the speed of sound is _______ proportional to the square-root of the Young's modulus of the medium.
The threshold of hearing is _______.
The minimum audible intensity is _______.
For humans with normal hearing, the upper limit of audible frequency is _______ Hz.
The _______ is the unit used for measuring the intensity level of sound.
An example of a _______ is the sound bouncing around in a large speaker.
Sound is a sequence of _______ waves.
The range of frequencies a person can hear is known as the range of _______.
The lower and upper limit of sound a normal human can here is called the limit of _______.
By using a _______ generator, the audible frequency range can be determined.
Some portable sound _______ generators can create sound frequencies 0 - 22 kHz.
Bats and dolphins use _______ in echolocation.
Most sonar technologies use _______.

_______ can be used to inspect metal pipes and measure the thickness of the wooden boards.
A prenatal _______ test uses high-frequency sound waves.
The device which transmits ultrasounds is called a _______.
_______ can be used to look at the inside of the abdomen.
With prenatal _______ the echoes are recorded and transformed into video or photographic images of the baby.
_______ scalers use ultrasound to remove calculus (plaque) deposits from the teeth.
_______ scalers work mechanically using high-frequency ultrasound that effectively blasts calculus.
The transmission of high-energy ultrasound may result in the creation of _______ bubbles.
Cavitation bubbles are created at the sites of _______.
_______ cleaning is especially effective in the cleaning of irregular surfaces.
The sonar is a type of technology that works based on _______.
_______ is used to detect the position of other vessels.
The wavelength of light from a star observed from the earth shifts due to the Doppler effect towards the _______ end when the earth and the star are moving away from each other.
The shift of wavelength towards the red end is called _______.
_______ ultrasound is a technique that is used to examine blood flow.
In medicine, the _______ measures the blood flow rates.
Blood flow rates can be found by measuring the Doppler shift in _______ reflected by red blood cells.

The _______ ultrasound can help reveal blockage to blood flow.
The _______ ultrasound can show the formation of plaque in arteries.
The _______ ultrasound can provide detailed information on the heartbeat of the fetus during labor and delivery.
The _______ ultrasound can be used to examine organs such as the heart (echocardiography) and kidney.
The timbre of a sound depends on its _______.
The waveform varies with the number of overtones, or _______.
In a solid medium, the speed of sound is _______ proportional to the mass density of the medium.
In a liquid medium, the speed of sound is _______ proportional to the mass density of the medium.
In a gas medium, the speed of sound is _______ proportional to the square-root of the adiabatic constant of the gas.
In a gas medium, the speed of sound is _______ proportional to the mass density of the gas.
The level of intensity of a sound is measured relative to a _______ intensity value.
The reference intensity value of sound is _______ W m^{-2}.
Echoes are produced where a sound wave is _______ from a large and hard surface.
An echo is the repetition of sound due to the _______ of sound.
The floors are covered with rugs and carpets to reduce the effect of _______.
A _______ is a reflection of light or sound waves, or a wide-reaching impact of an action.
_______ means able to be heard.
_______ means the ability to be heard.
_______ means the capability of detecting sounds in a certain range of frequencies.
